summary: Frontend-only verification for bug fix: Footer legal links are now clickable and route to /privacy, /terms, /cookies (LegalPage). All 3 pages render distinct titles and content (not blank, not 404). Footer links work from Home and /contact. Regression checks on primary nav routes (Home, About, Commodities, Partners, Careers, Contact) passed with header + footer visible and no page errors. Bug fix VERIFIED.

issues: Stats display $74M+, 148K+, 7+, 7+ instead of the $250M+, 500K+, 25+, 25+ mentioned in the review request. Likely a content/data change, not a bug — flagging so main agent can confirm intent.

critical_code_review_comments: LegalPage.jsx derives doc from pathname.replace('/', '') — works for /privacy, /terms, /cookies but would silently fall back to privacy content for any nested/malformed path (defensive default, acceptable).

Same <h1> element is reused across route changes (React key stays stable). Functionally fine, but test assertions relying on immediate text_content after navigation must wait for text to update. Purely a test concern, no user impact.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Legal pages verified: /privacy, /terms, /cookies with data-testid='legal-page'. Footer testids: footer-privacy-link, footer-terms-link, footer-cookies-link. Footer is present on all non-admin routes via Layout in App.js. Backend was fully covered in iteration_10 (29/29) and not re-run here.
verified_scenarios: Direct GET /privacy renders 'Privacy Policy' + body (872 chars)
Direct GET /terms renders 'Terms of Use' + body (723 chars)
Direct GET /cookies renders 'Cookie Policy' + body (602 chars)
Routes /, /about, /commodities, /partners, /careers, /contact all load with header+footer, zero page errors
